title: Audit current extension code for salvageable reality-grounded pieces
description: Determine whether the existing pi-grok-build code contains anything worth keeping. Classify each module/function/test as keep, rewrite, delete, or quarantine. Inputs include CURRENT_STATE.md, src/, test/, package metadata, and real Grok/Pi contracts. Acceptance: produce a written audit artifact with file-by-file salvage decisions and no implementation changes beyond documentation; create follow-up beads for any discovered gaps.
close_reason: Completed salvage audit in docs/audits/current-extension-salvage.md with file-by-file keep/rewrite/quarantine/delete decisions.

title: Define real Pi extension/provider contract across supported modes
description: Inspect Pi SDK/platform APIs and document exactly how providers/extensions are used in Pi modes such as -p, JSON, MCP/RCP/ACP/interactive if supported. Identify cost tracking, agent-turn, logging, error, tool, and model registration primitives. Acceptance: produce a contract document mapping each Pi surface to required extension behavior and evidence from actual Pi SDK/source, not guesses.
close_reason: Defined Pi mode/provider/tool/error/logging contract in docs/contracts/pi-platform-contract.md with evidence from local Pi SDK and CLI help.

title: Implement minimal JSONL Grok provider with PATH-only fast fail
description: Implement the first real provider mode using `grok` from PATH only, no private binary discovery. It must call Grok Build with model IDs from `grok models`, use streaming JSONL, parse real thought/text/end/error events, support reasoning output, disable Grok subagents by default, exclude best-of-n from scope, and surface Pi-idiomatic errors. Acceptance: `pi --model pi-grok-build/grok-build -p 'Respond exactly PI_GROK_OK'` visibly outputs PI_GROK_OK and invalid/missing grok failures are visible errors.
close_reason: Implemented PATH-only JSONL provider MVP and verified local Pi text/json acceptance with grok-build.

title: Build real test architecture: unit, fast-check property, and Pi e2e
description: Create three separate test suites: normal deterministic unit tests, property tests with fast-check, and actual e2e tests using local `.pi` or `tests/e2e/.pi` to import the extension and run Pi in -p and JSON modes asserting real output. Tests must not use logs as expectations. CI should run deterministic suites; real Grok/Pi e2e may be manual-gated if auth/local CLI is required. Acceptance: package scripts clearly separate suites and at least one e2e proves PI_GROK_OK through Pi runtime.
close_reason: Added separated unit/property/manual e2e test architecture with fast-check and real Pi text/json runtime acceptance.

title: Implement Pi-idiomatic logging, diagnostics, and error surfacing
description: Use Pi platform logging/debug primitives if available; otherwise integrate pino with debug/trace mode and lazy/guarded expensive logging. Surface provider failures through Pi idiomatic error events/messages, including missing grok on PATH, invalid model, auth failure, stream parse failure, ACP failure, and subprocess exit. Acceptance: debug/trace logs are useful for diagnosis but tests assert behavior, not log text.
close_reason: Added pino-backed diagnostics, redacted debug/trace logging, categorized Grok failure surfacing, best-effort extension registration, and tests/docs.

title: Implement Grok inspect, model discovery, reasoning, and provider metadata
description: Expose Grok inspect/model discovery in Pi tools/commands and provider registration. Model list must come from `grok models`, not hardcoded xAI assumptions. Reasoning/thought events must be represented according to Pi's actual content/event contract. Acceptance: `grok_inspect`/command works, models match CLI output, provider metadata is honest, and reasoning output is tested.
close_reason: Added honest provider metadata from , thinking level map, structured inspect details, command verification, and text-only provider input until image support is proven.

title: Release only after real Pi runtime acceptance across required modes
description: Perform final release verification across Pi modes required by the goal, including -p and JSON e2e assertions, any MCP/RCP/ACP/interactive surfaces Pi actually supports, package publication, live Pi settings/package update, and post-install smoke. Acceptance: completion audit maps every user requirement to concrete evidence before marking the goal done.

title: Run context research on dpcode and OpenCode Grok integrations
description: Use context-research over dpcode (https://github.com/Emanuele-web04/dpcode/releases/source as available) and OpenCode to understand existing Grok CLI integration patterns, edge cases, auth/session handling, JSONL/ACP behavior, and testing strategy. Acceptance: produce a research brief with concrete source references and recommendations for pi-grok-build design.
close_reason: Completed context research over dpcode and OpenCode in docs/research/dpcode-opencode-grok-integration.md.

title: Add selectable ACP integration mode with documented limitations
description: Add a second integration path using `grok agent stdio` ACP, selectable via env var or small config file. Compare behavior against JSONL mode and document tradeoffs: persistence, auth, latency, event model, tool behavior, session handling, and failure modes. Acceptance: both JSONL and ACP modes have explicit configuration, tests where feasible, and docs explaining when to choose each.
close_reason: Implemented selectable JSONL/ACP mode via PI_GROK_BUILD_MODE, added ACP stdio JSON-RPC bridge, tests, and docs. Verified npm run check, npm run test:ci, and PATH=/Users/ramarivera/.grok/bin:$PATH npm run test:e2e.

title: Add Biome lint/format and Effect TS conventions
description: Configure Biome for linting and formatting, wire package scripts, and refactor core logic toward Effect TS where it improves typed error handling, resource safety, and composability. Acceptance: `npm run lint`, `npm run format:check`, and typecheck pass; core provider/runtime errors are modeled without lazy throw/catch spaghetti.
close_reason: Added Biome lint/format/check scripts and config, formatted the codebase, added Effect dependency, and modeled Grok binary detection as an Effect API.

title: Ground and implement image/video generation Pi tools
description: Determine whether Grok CLI exposes image/video generation. If not, research where Grok stores usable auth tokens and whether direct xAI APIs can be called safely/legally from the extension without asking users to paste secrets. Implement Pi tools for image and video generation only after real endpoint/auth behavior is proven. Acceptance: tools work against real API/CLI or are explicitly not shipped; no fake advertised image/video surface remains.

title: Document scope, modes, limitations, and release criteria
description: Rewrite documentation so it reflects reality: supported Pi modes, JSONL vs ACP integration, unsupported/out-of-scope Grok subagents and best-of-n provider behavior, auth expectations, PATH expectations, tests, debug logging, and release gates. Acceptance: README/CURRENT_STATE or docs clearly state what works, what is experimental, and how to verify.
